articles.title: Microsoft Teams Relay Servers Abused in DragonForce Ransomware Attack
articles.summary: The attackers deployed a new Go-based backdoor that uses Microsoft Teams servers for command-and-control. The post Microsoft Teams Relay Servers Abused in DragonForce Ransomware Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: Breaches don't always start with a zero-day. An exposed admin panel can get brute-forced, or credentials reused from a previous attack. articles.title: CISA orders feds to patch max severity Joomla plugin flaw by Friday
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has ordered federal agencies to patch a maximum-severity flaw in the Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor (JCE) plugin that is being actively exploited in the wild. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft Working on Patch for ‘RoguePlanet’ Zero-Day
articles.summary: The public PoC code exploits a race condition in Microsoft Defender to spawn a command prompt with System privileges. The post Microsoft Working on Patch for ‘RoguePlanet’ Zero-Day appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft working on Defender patch for RoguePlanet zero-day
articles.summary: Microsoft confirmed that it's working on a security patch for a Defender zero-day vulnerability named "RoguePlanet," disclosed one week ago. [...]

articles.title: Chrome and Firefox Updated to Patch Critical, High-Severity Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: The browser updates address multiple memory safety bugs that could potentially lead to remote code execution. The post Chrome and Firefox Updated to Patch Critical, High-Severity Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: 145 Mastra npm Packages Compromised via Hijacked Contributor Account
articles.summary: As many as 145 npm packages associated with the Mastra namespace ("@mastra/*"), a popular open-source JavaScript and TypeScript framework for building artificial intelligence (AI) applications, have been compromised as part of a software supply chain attack codenamed easy-day-js, per findings from E

articles.title: Joomla, LiteSpeed Vulnerabilities Exploited in Attacks
articles.summary: The flaws allow attackers to execute arbitrary PHP code and gain root privileges on shared hosting servers. The post Joomla, LiteSpeed Vulnerabilities Exploited in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Kodak confirms data breach claimed by ShinyHunters extortion gang
articles.summary: Kodak has confirmed that it's working with external cybersecurity experts to investigate a security breach after hackers gained access to some of the company's data. [...]

articles.title: 3 Recently Patched Fortinet FortiSandbox Vulnerabilities in Hacker Crosshairs
articles.summary: SOCRadar has detected 30,000 compromised Fortinet firewalls that expose networks to hacking. The post 3 Recently Patched Fortinet FortiSandbox Vulnerabilities in Hacker Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: GitHub dismissed security reports on flaws now exploited by supply-chain worm, researchers say
articles.summary: GitHub rejected two formal vulnerability reports identifying design flaws that researchers say are enabling variants of the Shai-Hulud supply-chain worm to infect and compromise hundreds of software packages and developer accounts worldwide.

articles.title: New Rokarolla Android malware targets 217 banking, crypto apps
articles.summary: A new Android banking trojan named Rokarolla is targeting 217 banking and cryptocurrency applications using an extensive set of 137 commands. [...]

articles.title: Google Vertex AI SDK Flaw Let Attackers Hijack Model Uploads via Bucket Squatting
articles.summary: A flaw in the Google Cloud Vertex AI SDK for Python let an attacker with no access to a victim's project hijack the victim's machine learning model upload and run code inside Google's serving infrastructure. articles.title: Steam Workshop abused to spread malware via Wallpaper Engine app
articles.summary: Threat actors are abusing Steam Workshop, Valve's community hub for downloading game-related content, to push various malware hidden in wallpaper packages. [...]

articles.title: ClickFix Campaigns Expand Malware Delivery With New Loaders and Fake Update Lures
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged multiple ClickFix campaigns that deliver three malware loaders called BabaDeda Loader, Lorem Ipsum Loader, and Potemkin, per independent reports from Morphisec, BlueVoyant, and Huntress, respectively. articles.title: India temporarily blocks Telegram over medical exam cheating fears
articles.summary: Authorities said scammers previously exploited the feature by posting fake exam questions before the test and later replacing them with the real questions, making it look like they had leaked the exam in advance.

articles.title: iRhythm Confirms Data Stolen in Hack
articles.summary: The digital health company said it learned of the breach on June 8 and the attackers demanded a ransom. The post iRhythm Confirms Data Stolen in Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: UK to require ID or face scan before you can make social media accounts
articles.summary: Opening a new social media account in the UK will soon mean proving you're over 16 with an ID upload or a facial age scan, under a government ban on under-16s taking effect in spring 2027. Security experts warn the age checks are easy to circumvent and create new data-breach risks. [...]

articles.title: Hacker Conversations: Isira Adithya, the Evolution of an Ethical Hacker
articles.summary: From building LED bulbs to graduating college and buying a house with money earned from bug bounties. The post Hacker Conversations: Isira Adithya, the Evolution of an Ethical Hacker appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: GhostTree Attack Abused Recursive Windows Junctions to Hide Malware
articles.summary: GhostTree uses recursive NTFS junctions to generate vast numbers of valid Windows file paths. Varonis explains how the technique could cause Microsoft Defender folder scans to never complete, leaving malware undetected. [...]

articles.title: AI and Cybersecurity – Everything You Wanted to Know, But Were Afraid to Ask
articles.summary: From defending networks to enabling attacks, artificial intelligence is changing every aspect of cybersecurity. articles.summary: Security researchers at Zimperium's zLabs have documented a new Android banking trojan, Rokarolla, that targets 217 banking and cryptocurrency apps and packs 137 remote commands. articles.title: Cybercrime Group Claims Novo Nordisk Hack

articles.title: Flock Cameras Are Being Used for Stalking
articles.summary: There are over a dozen cases around the country where police officers are using the Flock surveillance camera system to obsessively and illegally stalk people. Alternate link.

articles.title: Atomic Arch Supply Chain Attack Hits 1,500 AUR Packages

articles.title: SimpleHelp bug lets hackers create rogue remote support accounts
articles.summary: A vulnerability in the SimpleHelp remote management software allows unauthenticated attackers to create privileged technician accounts on servers using the OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication protocol. [...]

articles.summary: A China-linked espionage group hid inside North American medical, academic, and military research networks for more than a year, quietly stealing sensitive research and defense email. articles.title: Maine closes data breach portal to the public after fake reports
articles.summary: Maine is still allowing companies to report breaches, but won’t make the portal easily available to the public until after it completes an audit of its procedures to stop such incidents, according to a press release from the Maine attorney general’s office.
